summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint passes a user-supplied URL directly to axios.get() with zero restrictions, enabling the n8n server to fetch arbitrary internal URLs and return the response body to the attacker.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the n8n server to fetch a URL pointing to an internal HTTP server (127.0.0.1:8888) and return the full response body including proof-of-concept exfiltration data to the authenticated attacker.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction caused the n8n server to fetch http://127.0.0.1:8888/secret-internal-endpoint via the from-url endpoint. The server returned HTTP 200 with the full internal response body containing the proof signal 'EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_INTERNAL_DATA_EXFILTRATED' and simulated secret metadata 'aws-access-key:AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE'. The internal server confirmed it received the GET request.
verification_method: The exploit starts a fake internal HTTP server that serves a JSON response containing a unique proof signal string 'EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_INTERNAL_DATA_EXFILTRATED'. After triggering the SSRF, the exploit checks if the proof signal appears in the from-url endpoint response. The internal server also logs the incoming request, providing a secondary confirmation that the server made the outbound request.
evidence_produced_by_run: The n8n server returned HTTP 200 from the from-url endpoint with the full internal server response body.
The proof signal 'EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_INTERNAL_DATA_EXFILTRATED' was present in the response, confirming data exfiltration through SSRF.
The internal server log confirmed that n8n made a GET request to /secret-internal-endpoint.
Simulated secret metadata 'aws-access-key:AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE' was exfiltrated through the SSRF response.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub at the n8n@2.4.0 tag and mounts it read-only at /target-repo. The exploit runs against the official n8n Docker image at the same version.
